title: The One With The Soap Opera Party

lines.speaker: Teleplay
lines.text: Andrew Reich & Ted Cohen

lines.speaker: Story
lines.text: Shana Goldberg-Meehan
Monica's apartment

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (enters the room) Hey, you guys, what are you doing tomorrow night?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (browsing through a diary) Well, let me see... I-I believe I'm... yes, falling asleep in front of the TV.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, my agent hooked me up with six tickets to a great play.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I could fall asleep at a play.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's a one-woman play called "Why don't you like me: a bitter woman's journey through life".

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It sounds interesting!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, it does sound interesting, I mean, to listen to a woman complain for two hours, I don't think it gets bett... (Ross starts snoring, faking to fall asleep)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know, I know, we can drive, we can vote, we can work, what more do these broads want?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You guys are gonna have a great time, I promise!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? How come that you don't have to go!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I wish I could but I just found out that I have to be at work really early the next day, so I can't go, but, you know, take the extra ticket and invite whoever you want.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (browsing through a diary) Uh, let's see, who do I hate?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (gets up from the sofa and moves to the kitchen but Joey blocks her way) Oh, sorry... Oops, sorry.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (lifts Rachel up and moves her behind him so she can walk on) Hey, here you go.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh... oooh... (pause) (Rachel is all bah-jiggity about Joey) oh, ah... (pause) (to Monica) Can I ask you a question?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Do you think it's possible for two friends to fool around and... and not have it be a big deal?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, I don't think it ever works. Why?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No reason.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, no, Rachel?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Who do you wanna fool around with?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (with high pitched voice) Nobody, forget it!
Monica points at Joey, Rachel turns and sees him

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (giggling and whispering) Maybe.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (whispering) You can't!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (whispering) Why? (Monica gesticulates mumbling something that starts with "because") Seriously I did not understand a word that you said.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: In the hall.
In the hall

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You wanna fool around with Joey?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! You know, ever since I had that dream about him, and can't get it out of my head! And what's the big deal, people do it all the time!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Who? Who do you know that are friends that just fool around?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok, off the top of my head... Don and Janet.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Who, who are they?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know them from work.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Both of them?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, one of them...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Which one?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know, what were the names I just said?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rachel, things can get incredibly complicated.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, all right, you're right, I won't do anything with Joey, I just thought that we (Joey enters the hall) Ok so that would be two cups of tarragon, one pound of baking soda and one red onion? (Joey enters his apartment)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What the hell are you cooking!
Opening credits
Central Perk, Ross enters

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, you guys won't believe what I have to do for work today.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, but, Ross you chose a career of talking about dinosaurs.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (covering with his hand Chandler's face, like pretending he's not there) (to Monica) There're these two professors who are joining my department and I have to meet them here and show them around campus.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What's so bad about that?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's I just know they're gonna be a couple of windbags wearing tweed jackets with suede elbow patches.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (fingering her elbow): Ross?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (looking his elbow, where there's a patch) These aren't suede.
a woman walks in

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: (to Gunther) Excuse me, I'm looking for someone. You don't, by any chance, know a Ross Geller?

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi, hi, I'm Ross Geller.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Oh, hi. I'm professor Wealer.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, oh, that's, that's, that's nice.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: It's a... It's good to meet you! Thank you so much for taking the time out to show me around.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, no, it's no big deal, I mean, if I weren't doing this I'd just, you know, be at the gym working out.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Chandler) Is he gonna introduce us?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Monica) No, I think we're just blurry shapes to him now.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: And, by the way, I really enjoyed your paper on the connection between geographic isolation and rapid mutagenesis.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, ha, I wrote that in one minute.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to chandler) Twenty bucks says they're married within the month.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (hitting Monica with his suitcase) (to Charlie) We should probably get going, you know, we got a lot of ground to cover.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Oh, ah, isn't there another professor that is supposed to come with us?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: I'm pretty sure, professor Spafford from Cornell?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, well he's obviously late and the rule in my class is "if you can't come on time, then don't come at all". (pause) An option that many of my students use. (pause) Shall we?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: You don't think we should wait for him?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You know what, he's a big boy, I'm sure he'll find us, ok?

lines.speaker: Professor Spafford
lines.text: Professor Geller?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, damn it!
Joey's apartment
Rachel enters the room and checks the answering machine

lines.speaker: Jane
lines.text: (from the answering machine) Hi Joey it's Jane Rogers, can't wait for your party tonight. Listen, I forgot your address, can you give me a call? Thanks, bye.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering the room) Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What's happenin'?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, it's a real shame you can't make it to that one-woman show tonight.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, I'd love to, but I gotta get up so early the next day and so, you know me, work comes first

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, yeah, yeah, yeah... (she plays the answering machine)

lines.speaker: Message
lines.text: "Hi Joey, it's Jane Rogers can't wait for your party tonight" (Joey's upset and stops it)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (yelling) Stupid Jane Rogers!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (angry) You are having a party tonight??

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Kinda have a... a thing for the Days Of Our Life's people.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And you weren't going to tell us? How did you think you were gonna get away with that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I do it every year.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You do that every year??

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I didn't have to tell you that!! I'm stupider than Jane Rogers!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, that's why you got these tickets to that play, to get rid of us??

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And last year is that why you sent us to that medieval times restaurant?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: OH! And the year before that, when you set up that nighttime tour of that button factory?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (Smiling) I can't believe you guys went for that one!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, why wouldn't you invite us to your parties?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You're fine, ok? But everyone else acts like an idiot around famous people!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, then so you just invite me...!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (speaking aloud) Please, I was trying to be nice, you're the worst one!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, Joey, come on! Please, please! Let me come, I will behave, I promise! I will behave! Please, please, please...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ok, ok! Fine! You can come, but don't tell anybody else. It's up on the roof at 8.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (yelling and jumping like a child) OH, a soap opera roof party!! I'm going to a soap opera roof party!! Oh my God, oh my God!! (realizing how she's behaving) And it's out of my system!
Ross, Charlie and Professor Spafford are sitting at the table in a restaurant

lines.speaker: Professor Spafford
lines.text: (speaking very slowly) And then my wife and I went on a cruise to the Galapagos. There was a sea food buffet you wouldn't believe. There were clams, and mussels, and oysters, and cracked crab, and snow crab, and king crab. It's a pity I'm allergic to shellfish.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (very bored, he tries to avoid the conversation speaking to Charlie) So, where did you get your undergraduate degree?

lines.speaker: Professor Spafford
lines.text: And that's not all I'm allergic to.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Charlie) Oh, it's not over!

lines.speaker: Professor Spafford
lines.text: I'm also allergic to peanuts, and cashews, and almonds, and filberts...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So basically all nuts?

lines.speaker: Professor Spafford
lines.text: Interestingly... no.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Kinda playing fast and loose with the word "interesting".

lines.speaker: Professor Spafford
lines.text: If you'll excuse me I'm going to use the restroom. (he goes away)

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Oh my God!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I've lost the will to live.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Let's ditch him!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Come on, he's still in the bathroom! I'm begging you!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh... ok, fine. But... ehm... I just have one question for you, ehm... (aping Professor Spafford) When we exit should we walk, or run, or prance, or stroll...

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Stop it, stop it! He talks slow but he might pee fast! Ok, let's go!! (they run outside)
Monica and Chandler's apartment

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering with Charlie) Oh, hey you guys! This is Charlie! Charlie, this is Phoebe and my sister, Monica.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, Charlie is gonna be joining my department.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, you're a paleonthologist, too! (pause) Oh, ok, now, what do you think of Ranion's new theory of species' variegation in segmented arthopods?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Well, I think he's a little out there, but he does have some interesting ideas...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ah, ah.

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: (her mobile phone rings) Oh, I'm sorry. I'll take this. Excuse me. (she cuts herself off for a moment)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Phoebe) Ranion's theory of species variegation?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I saw the article on your coffee table and I memorized the title to freak you out!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Ross) So, did you two have fun?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God, she's great! I mean, we-we have so much in common and she's just cool, and funny...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And I don't know if you've noticed but she's a (aloud) HOTTY!! (Charlie looks at her) HI!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (she enters wearing a bath-robe) Hey... Hi you guys! Listen, you know what? I'm not feeling really well. I think I can't get out for the play.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really? Wh-what's wrong?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know! I think it's kind of serious! Oh, you know... I was watching this thing on TV this morning about... Newcastle disease... and I think I might have it!!

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Oh, Newcastle disease is a secretion borne virus that only affects chickens and... other poultry.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: ... Ok, who is this?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm sorry, Rachel, this is Charlie Wealer, she's a collegue.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, hi! I would check your hand but... I'm sure you don't want to get my chicken disease!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, Rachel, Can-can I see you for a sec?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sure! (Monica takes her apart) Oh...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You're not sick!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? Yes, I am!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok, then, why are you... (she opens her robe revealing a nice black dress) all dressed up??

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: When you're sick, you do whatever you can to make yourself feel better! (she closes her robe)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You just wanna stay home so you can make a move on Joey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, no, no! I heard you before, that is so not what this is!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok, what is this?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok! (whispering) Joey is having a secret Days Of Our Lives party up on the roof and he sent you guys to the play to get rid of you!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (aloud) WHAT?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (looking at them) Wh-what's going on?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey is having a secret Days Of Our Lives party up on the roof!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And he didn't want you guys to know about it but I came over here to tell you!!

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: I thought you came to say you were sick.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok professor or detective?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Joey's having a party and he wasn't gonna invite us?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, and he does it every year! That's why he's sending you to that play! That's why he sent us to that medieval restaurant and to that button factory!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And that horrible museum tour!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I arranged that...
Joey enters, wearing a bathrobe

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (sounds tired) Hey you guys, I'm turning in. Have fun.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: We know about your party Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What party?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: The game's over! Take off your robe!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (looks perplexed and opens up his robe) Ok... I mean...

lines.speaker: Everyone
lines.text: No!! Cover it up!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Charlie after covering himself up again) Nice to meet ya! (Charlie waves hesitantly and Joey leaves)
The Roof

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (To some people) Hey! Hey alright! Hey, glad you could make it (Shakes a man's hand) Thanks for coming.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God! Kyle Lowder!

lines.speaker: Kyle Lowder
lines.text: (to Monica) Hi. (walks on)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (Yells after him) I love you!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, that's why I didn't invite you. you have to calm down, alright... go, go get yourself a drink or something...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh yeah that's what you want - my inhibitions lowered.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (Excited) Oh my God, can you believe we are surrounded by all this? I can barely control myself.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Monica, you might want to remember that you are married. Where is Chandler anyway? (Looks around)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (Shocked) Oh my God! Chandler!
The theater. Chandler is sitting in the otherwise empty front row, looking around nervously

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Where the hell is everybody?
The lights dim and Chandler tries to get away but as the bitter lady comes on stage and starts yelling he promptly changes his mind and sits down

lines.speaker: Bitter lady
lines.text: (yelling) Why don't you like me?! Chapter One: My first period.
The Roof, Rachel is talking to a guy who hands her a tissue with something written on it

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Rachel) Hey! Joey said no autographs! But if she's gettting one, then I want one too: To Monica. And none of this "best wishes" crap. I want "love".

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok actually Mon, Matthew was just giving me his phone number.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh man! If I had known I was coming to this party I never would have gotten married!

lines.speaker: Matthew Ashford
lines.text: It was nice to meet you Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nice to meet you.

lines.speaker: Matthew Ashford
lines.text: Call me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok
Matthew leaves

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (yelling after him) We will!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Rachel) Look at you with all the guys!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I guess you have forgotten all about Joey?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, well, I guess I have forgotten about Joey and clearly you've forgotten about Chandler!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Please... Chandler is the love of my life... (At which point a man in leather pants walks by)... oooh leather pants! Have Mercy! (Follows the man in the leather)
cut to Ross and Charlie

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Charlie) So, eh... it's probably gonna be hard for you to leave Boston, huh?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Actually, I'm kinda happy to be leaving... I just broke up with someeone.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ooh... so sad... Still, it can't be easy for you to leave Harvard? Especially after working alongside a Nobel Prize winner like Albert Wintermeyer?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Actually, Alby is the guy I broke up with.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You... you dated Albert Wintermeyer?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Yeah...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: ... And you called him Alby!? (laughs) I mean that's like... like calling Albert Einstein... er... Alby...

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Yeah, well, he is a brilliant man.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Eh, you think? I mean, you went out with a guy who improved the accuracy of radiocarbon dating by a factor of 10!

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Yes! And while that is everything one looks for in a boyfriend, he had a lot of issues...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (very interested) Oh! like what?! (Charlie looks at him confused, but smiling) Oh I'm sorry, I don't mean to pry... it's just that this must be what regular people experience when they watch "Access Hollywood".

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Ok, you want the dirt? Alby was seriously insecure. I mean, he was really intimidated by the guy I dated before him.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Who is intimidating to a guy who won the Nobel Prize?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: A guy who won two.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (a little suprised) Two? Wha...? Don't tell me you dated Benjamin Hobart

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: Yeah... for three years.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God! Have you ever been in a relationship with someone who hasn't won the Nobel Prize?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: (smiling and thinks for a moment)... no... bu but there was my first boyfriend Billy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh yeah? no, no Nobel Prizes for him?

lines.speaker: Charlie
lines.text: No, but he did just win the McArthur genius grant.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Huh... huh... what a loser! Some more wine? (takes the half-full glasses and goes to the counter)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey Ross!

lines.speaker: Rache
lines.text: How is it going with Charlie?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (sarcastically) Oh Great! After I finish my wine I'm going to blow my... eh. average-sized brains out.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, What's the matter?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: She... she only dates geniuses and Nobel Prize winners. Oh my God, at the chinese restaurant earlier today, I put chopsticks in my mouth and pretented to be a woolly mammoth.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I always loved that!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Of course you would, your brains are smaller than mine!! (Rachel nods) Man, I can't compete with the guys she goes out with, they are so out of my league! oh my God!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Worse?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh much, much worse. I did my impression of Joan Rivers as one of the earliest amphibians... (gestures with his hands and says in an impression voice?) "Can we walk"? (Phoebe starts laughing) Oh, you... you like that?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (still laughing) No. (points to Rachel who stuck two straws in her mouth to look like a mammoth's tusks)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? (turns around to look at Rachel)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Come on! I think this is funny! (Ross leaves)
Chandler in the theater

lines.speaker: Bitter lady
lines.text: Well, I bet you are all thinkin' "Now would be a really great time for an intermission", huh?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To himself) oh yes, God yes!!

lines.speaker: Bitter lady
lines.text: (now yelling) Well, you're not gonna get one! Because in life there are no intermissions, people. Chapter 7: Divorce is a 4 letter word. (Now standing right in front of Chandler and bending down almost to his level as if speaking to him, yelling even louder) How could he leave me?!?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I... I don't know... you seem lovely.
The roof, Joey is walking towards Ross with a guy

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Ross, this is one of my co-stars, Dirk. (To Dirk) Dirk, this is my good friend Ross. (Ross and Dirk shake hands)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nice to meet you.

lines.speaker: Dirk
lines.text: Hey! So what show are you on?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I'm no actor, I'm a professor of palaeontology.
Dirk is confused

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's a science.

lines.speaker: Dirk
lines.text: Oh! Hey well listen, I play a scientist on "Days". And my character has just won the Nobel prize.
Ross looks annoyed hearing that even Dirk's character won a Nobel prize

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Ross, listen, Dirk was wondering about the woman that you brought and if you guys were together, or...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, well no, but I mean, she only goes out with really, really smart guys.

lines.speaker: Dirk
lines.text: Hey, I got a 690 on my SATs.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'd lead with that.
Cut to Monica holding up her shirt, revealing her bra. An actor stands beside her, holding a pen in his hand.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's it, just sign right on the bra (the actor does so).

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Monica!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Don't worry Joe, I won't come next year!
Joey, resigned, walks to Rachel's table

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (To Rachel) What have you got there?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Just some boys gave me their phone numbers.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah, let me see! (she hands him a pile of tissues) Damn, that's a lot of guys! Are you a little slutty?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (drunken voice) I think I am.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (browsing the tissues) Let me see if I approve any of these clowns. This guy wears a rug (discards one). This guy's Canadian (discards another). And this guy is in a cult, ok, and it costs you 5,000$ to get to level three and I don't feel any different.
Rachel is puzzled

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (discarding all the remaining tissues one by one) Pass, pass, oh, pass, double-pass, pass...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (picking up the tissues) Why, why, what's wrong with these guys?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nothing major, it's just that, you know, they're not really good enough for you, and you deserve the best.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, you're so sweet.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's true. But you know what, it doesn't matter because I already know who you're gonna go home with tonight.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Who (looks around)?
Joey motions for Rachel to lean in. She does so.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (surprised) What? (with a nervous smile) Really?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes, 'cause we live together, that's a joke!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Screw it, I didn't get it! (they high-five)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Gotcha.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, Very funny... Joey.
Joey leaves. Rachel gulps down what's left of her drink and grimaces.
Chandler walks in

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Monica) So, how did you enjoy the play?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my god, honey, I'm so so so so so sorry.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well you should be. You missed the most powerful three hours in the history of the theater.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You really liked it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah! I mean at first I hated it, but why wouldn't I, because as a man I've been trained (bitter woman's tone) not to listen! (pause) But after chapter 16: "fat, single and ready to mingle", I was uplifted.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh really!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah, I had no idea the amazing journey you go through as a woman! Tell me, tell me about your first period!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Did somebody sign your bra?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So I got it when I was 13...
At the counter. Ross is sitting there, drinking. Phoebe approaches him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey Ross! So listen, about you and the dinosaur girl, are you really just gonna let a couple of Nobel prizes scare you off? What is that, come on, a piece of paper?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's actually a 1,000,000$ prize.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Go Charlie! But my point is, ok so she dated them but she also broke up with them. Maybe she's looking to, you know, slum it with some average Joe Phd.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, maybe. I do have my whole career in front of me. I mean, I can still win a Nobel prize. Although the last two papers I've written were widely discredited.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You're so much more than just brains! You're sweet, and kind, and funny...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And sexy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok well give her a chance to see all of that!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, you're right, thanks Pheebs, I'm gonna go find her.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Good for you! And hey, I thought your paper on punctuated equilibrium in the Devonian era was top notch!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Stop going through my stuff (walks away)!
Rachel approaches Monica

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Monica) Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I just wanted to let you know I've changed my mind: I'm gonna do it, I'm gonna kiss Joey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, you can't! Friends hooking up is a bad idea.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Please, what about you and Chandler?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's different! I was drunk and stupid!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well hello (points to herself)!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What about all the guys that you've got the phone numbers from? Why don't you just kiss one of them?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I could, I could but I don't want to! I want to kiss Joey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Alright (shrugs). I think it's a big mistake but it's your decision.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (pause) I'm gonna do it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And I can't stop you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No.
Rachel goes look for Joey. She sees him and Charlie kissing passionately.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Rachel) Hey Rach, have you seen Charlie anywhere?
Rachel just stands there staring at Joey and Charlie in silence. After a while, Ross turns around and sees them.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm smarter than him!
Closing credits
The theater. Monica, Phoebe and Chandler are sitting in the first line

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, thank you so much for these tickets, Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh well, this was a really important experience for me, and I wanted to share it with you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, you're so wonderful.

lines.speaker: Bitter woman
lines.text: Why don't you like me! Chapter one: my first period.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Can't believe you guys bought that, enjoy your slow death (runs away).
